generatedSentence: Elara, a seasoned screenwriter in her late fifties, felt a profound isolation. Her latest artwork, a scathing critique of the city's controlling oligarch, was met with chilling silence. The entire environment had become unpleasant, a suffocating blanket of corporate control. She often remembered her early internship, a time when the world seemed full of rainbow possibilities, not the grim legacy of power she now witnessed. She’d seen too many failures, too many dissenters ejected from public life. Her old friend, a former jester turned activist, warned her the ruling class was a viper in plain sight. Her small apartment, filled with the aroma of lentil soup, was her only fort. She sometimes dreamed of a daytrip by kayak on aqua waters, a pretty memory of freedom. Her deeper hunger for truth intensified. She possessed a utility belt of knowledge, a kit of meticulously researched facts, but felt constantly at a peak of unease, her want for exposure growing, even if it meant she might pay a heavy price. The headline screamed of a new dictator decree, designed to sicken public morale and enforce a new wave of deprivation. Elara saw it as a calculated move to keep the populace as vassal to the system. She knew several actors who were secretly planning to nominate an independent candidate, but the regime’s surveillance bandwidth made any open interaction dangerous. She often recalled the harrowing voyages of her ancestor, an oarsman who escaped a similar tyranny. This new decree felt like a personal disgrace, twisting the city into a simplistic narrative of control. The very air was volatile, thick with unspoken threats. The winds of change felt distant. Whispers of a secret resistance, led by a former jock now an avowed freedom builder, gave her a fleeting grip of hope. But the system was rigged, built on sinning and deceit. The dreadnought of state power loomed. She knew this was her threshold to act, her moment to draw a line. The thought of confronting such power made her feel overwrought. Elara formulated a daring plan: to inject truth into the city's market. She realized a group of newlywed hackers, a nihilistic yet brilliant duo, had been cajoled by the regime but could be swayed. She found them in a derelict hangars district, amidst old trains, sharing a bottle of whiskey. Their identity was hidden behind screens, but their morality was not entirely lost. She presented them with a detailed letter exposing the city’s deep corruption, an exposé that would magnify the regime's treachery. The subject of her investigation encompassed the entire orange system. This information, once released, would hit like a kinetic force, a ball of truth that could not be stopped. She knew this act of defiance would make her a runaway target, potentially injured beyond repair. The hackers, initially reluctant, finally objected to the regime's control. Their decision was a turning point, akin to opening Pandora's box, a desperate gamble for freedom. She felt a strange nervine calm as she handed over the baton of information, knowing the next move was theirs. This was the payback for years of oppression, a relentless trojans horse poised to breach their defenses. The howling storm of public outcry that followed the data drop was immense, a cascade of information hitting like thrice-repeated thunder. The corrupt regime, now exposed, was in complete trouble. Elara watched the fallout on a widescreen monitor, a sense of quiet triumph mixed with exhaustion. The hackers, now free of their burden, left their backpacks behind and boarded a tram, looking forward to a simpler existence. She thought of her adult grandson, a man she hoped would live in a better world, free from the rickets of systemic corruption. The field of journalism felt revitalized; no longer would they administer only carefully curated adjectives. The regime tried to reapply control, but the information had been harvested too widely, like an invasive forestry fire. It was a gain for truth, a victory for the people. A former wagoners boss, now a free citizen, openly sang karaoke in the streets, a symbol of newfound freedom. Elara, feeling the weight of her helmet of responsibility lift, decided to lay down her guard and retire. Perhaps she'd visit an otter sanctuary, or enjoy an oyster feast. She'd achieved her immortal goal, proving that even a small hedgehog can cause big hits. The echoes of the nightingale of freedom finally sang, no longer would the powerful hijacks the narrative. She had ridden the moral horse to victory.
panelSentences: Elara, a seasoned screenwriter in her late fifties, felt a profound isolation. Her latest artwork, a scathing critique of the city's controlling oligarch, was met with chilling silence. The entire environment had become unpleasant, a suffocating blanket of corporate control. She often remembered her early internship, a time when the world seemed full of rainbow possibilities, not the grim legacy of power she now witnessed. She’d seen too many failures, too many dissenters ejected from public life. Her old friend, a former jester turned activist, warned her the ruling class was a viper in plain sight. Her small apartment, filled with the aroma of lentil soup, was her only fort. She sometimes dreamed of a daytrip by kayak on aqua waters, a pretty memory of freedom. Her deeper hunger for truth intensified. She possessed a utility belt of knowledge, a kit of meticulously researched facts, but felt constantly at a peak of unease, her want for exposure growing, even if it meant she might pay a heavy price.

The headline screamed of a new dictator decree, designed to sicken public morale and enforce a new wave of deprivation. Elara saw it as a calculated move to keep the populace as vassal to the system. She knew several actors who were secretly planning to nominate an independent candidate, but the regime’s surveillance bandwidth made any open interaction dangerous. She often recalled the harrowing voyages of her ancestor, an oarsman who escaped a similar tyranny. This new decree felt like a personal disgrace, twisting the city into a simplistic narrative of control. The very air was volatile, thick with unspoken threats. The winds of change felt distant. Whispers of a secret resistance, led by a former jock now an avowed freedom builder, gave her a fleeting grip of hope. But the system was rigged, built on sinning and deceit. The dreadnought of state power loomed. She knew this was her threshold to act, her moment to draw a line. The thought of confronting such power made her feel overwrought.

Elara formulated a daring plan: to inject truth into the city's market. She realized a group of newlywed hackers, a nihilistic yet brilliant duo, had been cajoled by the regime but could be swayed. She found them in a derelict hangars district, amidst old trains, sharing a bottle of whiskey. Their identity was hidden behind screens, but their morality was not entirely lost. She presented them with a detailed letter exposing the city’s deep corruption, an exposé that would magnify the regime's treachery. The subject of her investigation encompassed the entire orange system. This information, once released, would hit like a kinetic force, a ball of truth that could not be stopped. She knew this act of defiance would make her a runaway target, potentially injured beyond repair. The hackers, initially reluctant, finally objected to the regime's control. Their decision was a turning point, akin to opening Pandora's box, a desperate gamble for freedom. She felt a strange nervine calm as she handed over the baton of information, knowing the next move was theirs. This was the payback for years of oppression, a relentless trojans horse poised to breach their defenses.

The howling storm of public outcry that followed the data drop was immense, a cascade of information hitting like thrice-repeated thunder. The corrupt regime, now exposed, was in complete trouble. Elara watched the fallout on a widescreen monitor, a sense of quiet triumph mixed with exhaustion. The hackers, now free of their burden, left their backpacks behind and boarded a tram, looking forward to a simpler existence. She thought of her adult grandson, a man she hoped would live in a better world, free from the rickets of systemic corruption. The field of journalism felt revitalized; no longer would they administer only carefully curated adjectives. The regime tried to reapply control, but the information had been harvested too widely, like an invasive forestry fire. It was a gain for truth, a victory for the people. A former wagoners boss, now a free citizen, openly sang karaoke in the streets, a symbol of newfound freedom. Elara, feeling the weight of her helmet of responsibility lift, decided to lay down her guard and retire. Perhaps she'd visit an otter sanctuary, or enjoy an oyster feast. She'd achieved her immortal goal, proving that even a small hedgehog can cause big hits. The echoes of the nightingale of freedom finally sang, no longer would the powerful hijacks the narrative. She had ridden the moral horse to victory.
rawPanelSentences: Elara, a seasoned **screenwriter** in her late fifties, felt a profound **isolation**. Her latest **artwork**, a scathing critique of the city's controlling **oligarch**, was met with chilling silence. The entire **environment** had become **unpleasant**, a suffocating blanket of corporate control. She often remembered her early **internship**, a time when the world seemed full of **rainbow** possibilities, not the grim **legacy** of power she now witnessed. She’d seen too many **failures**, too many dissenters **ejected** from public life. Her old friend, a former **jester** turned activist, warned her the ruling class was a **viper** in plain sight. Her small apartment, filled with the aroma of **lentil** soup, was her only **fort**. She sometimes dreamed of a **daytrip** by **kayak** on **aqua** waters, a **pretty** memory of freedom. Her deeper **hunger** for truth intensified. She possessed a **utility** belt of knowledge, a **kit** of meticulously researched facts, but felt constantly at a **peak** of unease, her **want** for exposure growing, even if it meant she might **pay** a heavy price.

The **headline** screamed of a new **dictator** decree, designed to **sicken** public morale and enforce a new wave of **deprivation**. Elara saw it as a calculated move to keep the populace as **vassal** to the system. She knew several **actors** who were secretly planning to **nominate** an independent candidate, but the regime’s surveillance **bandwidth** made any open **interaction** dangerous. She often recalled the harrowing **voyages** of her ancestor, an **oarsman** who escaped a similar tyranny. This new decree felt like a personal **disgrace**, twisting the city into a **simplistic** narrative of control. The very air was **volatile**, thick with unspoken threats. The **winds** of change felt distant. Whispers of a secret resistance, led by a former **jock** now an **avowed** freedom **builder**, gave her a fleeting **grip** of hope. But the system was rigged, built on **sinning** and deceit. The **dreadnought** of state power loomed. She knew this was her **threshold** to act, her moment to **draw** a line. The thought of confronting such power made her feel **overwrought**.

Elara formulated a daring plan: to **inject** truth into the city's **market**. She realized a group of **newlywed** hackers, a **nihilistic** yet brilliant duo, had been **cajoled** by the regime but could be swayed. She found them in a derelict **hangars** district, amidst old **trains**, sharing a bottle of **whiskey**. Their **identity** was hidden behind screens, but their **morality** was not entirely lost. She presented them with a detailed **letter** exposing the city’s deep corruption, an exposé that would **magnify** the regime's treachery. The **subject** of her investigation encompassed the entire **orange** system. This information, once released, would hit like a **kinetic** force, a **ball** of truth that could not be stopped. She knew this act of defiance would make her a **runaway** target, potentially **injured** beyond repair. The hackers, initially reluctant, finally **objected** to the regime's control. Their decision was a turning point, akin to opening **Pandora**'s box, a desperate gamble for freedom. She felt a strange **nervine** calm as she handed over the **baton** of information, knowing the next move was theirs. This was the **payback** for years of oppression, a relentless **trojans** horse poised to breach their defenses.

The **howling** storm of public outcry that followed the data drop was immense, a cascade of information hitting like **thrice**-repeated thunder. The corrupt regime, now exposed, was in complete **trouble**. Elara watched the fallout on a **widescreen** monitor, a sense of quiet triumph mixed with exhaustion. The hackers, now free of their burden, left their **backpacks** behind and boarded a **tram**, looking forward to a simpler existence. She thought of her adult **grandson**, a man she hoped would live in a better world, free from the **rickets** of systemic corruption. The **field** of journalism felt revitalized; no longer would they **administer** only carefully curated **adjectives**. The regime tried to **reapply** control, but the information had been **harvested** too widely, like an invasive **forestry** fire. It was a **gain** for truth, a victory for the people. A former **wagoners** boss, now a free citizen, openly sang **karaoke** in the streets, a symbol of newfound freedom. Elara, feeling the weight of her **helmet** of responsibility lift, decided to **lay** down her guard and retire. Perhaps she'd visit an **otter** sanctuary, or enjoy an **oyster** feast. She'd achieved her **immortal** goal, proving that even a small **hedgehog** can cause big **hits**. The echoes of the **nightingale** of freedom finally sang, no longer would the powerful **hijacks** the narrative. She had ridden the moral **horse** to victory.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a woman in her late fifties with sharp, intelligent eyes, prominent laugh lines, and a severe silver bob haircut, wearing a practical, worn trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A close-up of Elara, her face etched with weariness and resolve, clutching a well-worn notebook in her cluttered apartment, low angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A wide shot depicting the sprawling, oppressive cityscape under a dark, stormy sky, with towering, monolithic buildings casting long shadows, a lone, tiny figure (Elara) looking up from below, worm's eye view. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara, standing tall and resolute, handing a data chip to a shadowy figure (one of the adult hackers with a distinct mature feature like a goatee) in a dimly lit, industrial hangar filled with rusting train cars, eye-level shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A vibrant, bustling street scene filled with diverse, adult people celebrating, some singing karaoke, confetti in the air, Elara observing from a distance with a subtle, knowing smile, high angle shot. No text, no speech bubbles, no dialogue, no children.

definitions.definition: To wound or cause physical harm to a living creature.
To damage or impair.
To do injustice to.
To make ill.

definitions.definition: To become ill.
definitions.example: I will sicken if I don’t get some more exercise.

definitions.definition: To fill with disgust or abhorrence.
definitions.example: His arrogant behaviour sickens me.

(especially Russia, USA, Europe, or China) A very rich person, particularly with political power; a plutocrat.
(cosmogony) A protoplanet formed during oligarchic accretion.

The fruit of an orange tree; a citrus fruit with a slightly sour flavour.
The colour of a ripe fruit of an orange tree, midway between red and yellow.
Orange juice.

Orange coloured and flavoured soft drink.
To color orange.
To become orange.
Having the colour of the fruit of an orange tree; yellowred; reddish-yellow.

One who acts; a doer.

An advocate or proctor in civil courts or causes.
One who institutes a suit; plaintiff or complainant.

The entity that performs a role (in use case analysis).
(grammar) The most agent-like argument of a clause, e.g. 'the torpedo' in "The torpedo sank the boat" and "The torpedo fired".
To name someone as a candidate for a particular role or position, including that of an office.
To entitle, confer a name upon.
definitions.example: 1658: the City of Norwich [...] was enlarged, builded and nominated by the Saxons. — Sir Thomas Browne, Urne-Burial (Penguin 2005, p. 12)

definitions.definition: To answer; to address.

definitions.definition: To defeat.

definitions.definition: To execute research (in the field).

definitions.definition: To deploy in the field.

definitions.definition: The act of gaining; acquisition.
What is gained.
The factor by which a signal is multiplied.
To acquire possession of.
definitions.example: Looks like you've gained a new friend.

definitions.definition: To have or receive advantage or profit; to acquire gain; to grow rich; to advance in interest, health, or happiness; to make progress.
definitions.example: The sick man gains daily.

definitions.definition: To come off winner or victor in; to be successful in; to obtain by competition.
definitions.example: to gain a battle; to gain a case at law

definitions.definition: To increase.
To be more likely to catch or overtake an individual.
definitions.example: I'm gaining (on you).

definitions.definition: To reach.
definitions.example: to gain the top of a mountain

definitions.definition: To draw into any interest or party; to win to one's side; to conciliate.
To put on weight.
definitions.example: I've been gaining.

definitions.definition: (of a clock or watch) To run fast.
To take hold of, particularly with the hand.
definitions.example: After a few slips, the tires gripped the pavement.

definitions.definition: To help or assist, particularly in an emotional sense.
definitions.example: He grips me.

definitions.definition: To do something with another that makes you happy/gives you relief.
definitions.example: Let’s grip (get a coffee, hang, take a break, see a movie, etc.)

definitions.definition: To trench; to drain.
The situation or occurrence in which two or more objects or events act upon one another to produce a new effect; the effect resulting from such a situation or occurrence.
definitions.example: Be aware of interactions between different medications.

definitions.definition: A conversation or exchange between people.
definitions.example: I enjoyed the interaction with a bunch of like-minded people.

definitions.definition: A job taken by a student in order to learn a profession or trade.
The compound water.
A shade of colour, usually a mix of blue and green similar to the colour turquoise.
Of a greenish-blue colour.
A type of small boat, covered over by a surface deck, powered by the occupant or occupants using a double-bladed paddle in a sitting position, from a hole in the surface deck
To use a kayak, to travel or race in a kayak.
definitions.example: Kayaking is an Olympic sport.

definitions.definition: To traverse (a body of water) by kayak.
definitions.example: On a dare, he kayaked the Harlem River in New York from Hell's Gate to Spyten Duyvil.

definitions.definition: A circular wooden vessel, made of hooped staves.
A kind of basket made especially from straw of rushes, especially for holding fish; by extension, the contents of such a basket or similar container, used as a measure of weight.
definitions.example: 1961 18 Jan, Guardian (cited after OED):

definitions.definition: A collection of items forming the equipment of a soldier, carried in a knapsack.
Any collection of items needed for a specific purpose, especially for use by a workman, or personal effects packed for travelling.
definitions.example: Always carry a good first-aid kit.

definitions.definition: A collection of parts sold for the buyer to assemble.
definitions.example: I built the entire car from a kit.

definitions.definition: The set of skills and abilities chosen for a playable character.
The standard set of clothing, accessories and equipment worn by players.
Clothing.
definitions.example: Get your kit off and come to bed.

definitions.definition: A full software distribution, as opposed to a patch or upgrade.
A drum kit.
To assemble or collect something into kits or sets or to give somebody a kit. See also kit out and other derived phrases.
definitions.example: We need to kit the parts for the assembly by Friday, so that manufacturing can build the tool.

definitions.definition: A liquor distilled from the fermented mash of grain (as rye, corn, or barley).
A drink of whiskey.
A light gig or carriage; a tim-whiskey.
The letter W in the ICAO spelling alphabet.
A desire, wish, longing.
(often followed by of) Lack, absence.
Poverty.
Something needed or desired; a thing of which the loss is felt.
A depression in coal strata, hollowed out before the subsequent deposition took place.
To wish for or desire (something); to feel a need or desire for; to crave or demand.
definitions.example: What do you want to eat?  I want you to leave.  I never wanted to go back to live with my mother.

definitions.definition: (in particular) To wish, desire or demand to see, have the presence of or do business with.
definitions.example: Danish police want him for embezzlement.

definitions.definition: To desire (to experience desire); to wish.
definitions.example: You can leave if you want.

definitions.definition: (usually second person, often future tense) To be advised to do something (compare should, ought).
definitions.example: You’ll want to repeat this three or four times to get the best result.

definitions.definition: (now colloquial) To lack and be in need of or require (something, such as a noun or verbal noun).
definitions.example: That chair wants fixing.

definitions.definition: (now rare) To have occasion for (something requisite or useful); to require or need.
To be lacking or deficient or absent.
definitions.example: There was something wanting in the play.

definitions.definition: To be in a state of destitution; to be needy; to lack.
definitions.example: The paupers desperately want.

definitions.definition: To lack and be without, to not have (something).
definitions.example: She wanted anything she needed.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To lack and (be able to) do without.
Not pleasant.
The state or condition of being useful; usefulness.
Something that is useful.
The ability of a commodity to satisfy needs or wants; the satisfaction experienced by the consumer of that commodity.
Well-being, satisfaction, pleasure, or happiness.
A service provider, such as an electric company or water company; or, the securities of such a provider.
A software program designed to perform a single task or a small range of tasks, often to help manage and tune computer hardware, an operating system or application software.
definitions.example: I've bought a new disk utility that can recover deleted files.

definitions.definition: The ability to play multiple positions.
Having to do with, or owned by, a service provider.
definitions.example: utility line; utility bill

definitions.definition: Designating of a room in a house or building where mechanical equipment is installed; such as a furnace, water tank/heater, circuit breaker, and/or air conditioning unit; and often equipped with hookups for laundry equipment (washer/dryer).
definitions.example: utility room

definitions.definition: A totalitarian leader of a country, nation, or government.
definitions.example: Dictators are always punished eventually.

definitions.definition: A magistrate without colleague in republican Ancient Rome, who held full executive authority for a term granted by the senate (legislature), typically to conduct a war.
A tyrannical boss or authority figure.
A person who dictates text (e.g. letters to a clerk).
The result of a contest that neither side has won; a tie.
definitions.example: The game ended in a draw.

definitions.definition: The procedure by which the result of a lottery is determined.
definitions.example: The draw is on Saturday.

definitions.definition: Something that attracts e.g. a crowd.
The result of a two-innings match in which at least one side did not complete all their innings before time ran out (as distinguished from a tie).
A golf shot that (for the right-handed player) curves intentionally to the left. See hook, slice, fade.
A shot that is intended to land gently in the house (the circular target) without knocking out other stones; cf. takeout.
A dry stream bed that drains surface water only during periods of heavy rain or flooding.
A bag of cannabis.
Cannabis.
In a commission-based job, an advance on future (potential) commissions given to an employee by the employer.
A situation in which one or more players has four cards of the same suit or four out of five necessary cards for a straight and requires a further card to make their flush or straight.
The act of pulling back the strings in preparation of firing.
The spin or twist imparted to a ball etc. by a drawing stroke.
To move or develop something.
To exert or experience force.
(fluidic) To remove or separate or displace.
To change in size or shape.
To attract or be attracted.
(usually as draw on or draw upon) To rely on; utilize as a source.
definitions.example: She had to draw upon her experience to solve the problem.

definitions.definition: To disembowel.
To end a game in a draw (with neither side winning).
definitions.example: We drew last time we played.  I drew him last time I played him.  I drew my last game against him.

definitions.definition: To choose by means of a random selection process.
To make a shot that lands gently in the house (the circular target) without knocking out other stones.
To play (a short-length ball directed at the leg stump) with an inclined bat so as to deflect the ball between the legs and the wicket.
To hit (the ball) with the toe of the club so that it is deflected toward the left.
To strike (the cue ball) below the center so as to give it a backward rotation which causes it to take a backward direction on striking another ball.
A symbol in an alphabet.
definitions.example: There are twenty-six letters in the English alphabet.

definitions.definition: A written or printed communication, generally longer and more formal than a note.
definitions.example: I wrote a letter to my sister about my life.

definitions.definition: The literal meaning of something, as distinguished from its intended and remoter meaning (often contrasted with the spirit).
(plural) Literature.
definitions.example: Benjamin Franklin was multiskilled – a scientist, politician and a man of letters.

definitions.definition: A division unit of a piece of law marked by a letter of the alphabet.
A size of paper, 8½ in × 11 in (215.9 mm × 279.4 mm, US paper sizes rounded to the nearest 5 mm).
A size of paper, 215 mm × 280 mm.
A single type; type, collectively; a style of type.
To print, inscribe, or paint letters on something.
(scholastic) To earn a varsity letter (award).

An identifying initial of the academic institution that is awarded to a member of a varsity team. It is made from structured cloth, and intended to be sewn to a piece of clothing such as a varsity sweater or varsity jacket.
A passenger vehicle for public use that runs on tracks in the road (called a streetcar or trolley in North America).
A similar vehicle for carrying materials.
A people mover.
An aerial cable car.
A train with wheels that runs on a road; a trackless train.
A car on a horse railway or tramway (horse trams preceded electric trams).
The shaft of a cart.
One of the rails of a tramway.
To operate, or conduct the business of, a tramway.
To travel by tram.
To transport (material) by tram.
To align a component in mechanical engineering or metalworking, particularly the head of a drill press.
To apply again.
An act of revenge.
definitions.example: They beat us last year, so this year's win was payback.

definitions.definition: A benefit, reward, a form of recompense.
A return on investment
A refund, reimbursement
The surroundings of, and influences on, a particular item of interest.
The natural world or ecosystem.
All the elements that affect a system or its inputs and outputs.
A particular political or social setting, arena or condition.
The software and/or hardware existing on any particular computer system.
definitions.example: That program uses the Microsoft Windows environment.

definitions.definition: The environment of a function at a point during the execution of a program is the set of identifiers in the function's scope and their bindings at that point.
The set of variables and their values in a namespace that an operating system associates with a process.
A fortified defensive structure stationed with troops.
Any permanent army post.
An outlying trading-station, as in British North America.
To create a fort, fortifications, a strong point, or a redoubt.
A chemical or compound that changes into a gas easily.
Evaporating or vaporizing readily under normal conditions.
(of a substance) Explosive.
(of a price etc) Variable or erratic.
(of a person) Quick to become angry or violent.
Fickle.
Temporary or ephemeral.
(of a situation) Potentially violent.
(of a variable) Having its associated memory immediately updated with any changes in value.
(of memory) Whose content is lost when the computer is powered down
Passing through the air on wings, or by the buoyant force of the atmosphere; flying; having the power to fly.
The grantee of a fief, feud, or fee; one who keeps land of a superior, and who vows fidelity and homage to him, normally a lord of a manor; a feudatory; a feudal tenant.
A subordinate
To treat as a vassal or to reduce to the position of a vassal; to subject to control; to enslave.
To subordinate to someone or something.
Resembling a vassal; slavish; servile.
Overly simple.
In a manner that simplifies a concept or issue so that its nuance and complexity are lost or important details are overlooked.
Of or relating to simples, or medicinal herbs.

A knapsack, sometimes mounted on a light frame, but always supported by straps, worn on a person’s back for the purpose of carrying things, especially when hiking, or on a student's back when carrying books.
A similarly placed item containing a parachute or other life-support equipment.
To hike and camp overnight in backcountry with one's gear carried in a backpack
To engage in low-cost, generally urban, travel with minimal luggage and frugal accommodations
To place or carry (an item or items) in a backpack
The width, usually measured in hertz, of a frequency band.
Of a signal, the width of the smallest frequency band within which the signal can fit.
The rate of data flow in digital networks typically measured in bits per second; the bitrate.
The capacity, energy or time required.
definitions.example: I think it's a worthy project, but I just don't have the bandwidth right now.

definitions.definition: To persuade someone to do something which they are reluctant to do, especially by flattery or promises; to coax.

A form of entertainment popular in clubs, at parties, etc, in which individual members of the public sing along to pre-recorded instrumental versions of popular songs, the lyrics of which are displayed for the singer on a screen in time with the music.
A karaoke session.
To perform karaoke
Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.
definitions.example: As they accelerated onto the motorway, the wind tore the plywood off the car's roof-rack.

definitions.definition: Air artificially put in motion by any force or action.
definitions.example: the wind of a cannon ball;  the wind of a bellows

definitions.definition: The ability to breathe easily.
definitions.example: After the second lap he was already out of wind.

definitions.definition: News of an event, especially by hearsay or gossip. (Used with catch, often in the past tense.)
definitions.example: Steve caught wind of Martha's dalliance with his best friend.

definitions.definition: One of the five basic elements in Indian and Japanese models of the Classical elements).
Flatus.
definitions.example: Eww. Someone just passed wind.

definitions.definition: Breath modulated by the respiratory and vocal organs, or by an instrument.
The woodwind section of an orchestra. Occasionally also used to include the brass section.
A direction from which the wind may blow; a point of the compass; especially, one of the cardinal points, which are often called the "four winds".
Types of playing-tile in the game of mah-jongg, named after the four winds.
A disease of sheep, in which the intestines are distended with air, or rather affected with a violent inflammation. It occurs immediately after shearing.
Mere breath or talk; empty effort; idle words.
A bird, the dotterel.
The region of the solar plexus, where a blow may paralyze the diaphragm and cause temporary loss of breath or other injury.
The act of winding or turning; a turn; a bend; a twist.
The condition of being out of favor; loss of favor, regard, or respect.
The state of being dishonored, or covered with shame.
definitions.example: Now she lives in disgrace.

definitions.definition: Something which brings dishonor; the cause of reproach or shame; great discredit.
definitions.example: His behaviour at the party was a total disgrace! He was leeching on all the ladies, and insulting the men.

definitions.definition: An act of unkindness; a disfavor.
To put someone out of favor; to bring shame or ignominy upon.
To bring in a harvest; reap; glean.
To be occupied bringing in a harvest
definitions.example: Harvesting is a stressing, thirsty occupation

definitions.definition: To win, achieve a gain.
definitions.example: The rising star harvested well-deserved acclaim, even an Oscar under 21

definitions.definition: The bottom-most part of a doorway that one crosses to enter; a sill.
(by extension) An entrance; the door or gate of a house.
(by extension) Any end or boundary.
The outset of something; the point of entry, or the beginning of an action.
The start of the landing area of a runway.
The quantitative point at which an action is triggered, especially a lower limit.
The wage or salary at which income tax becomes due.
The point where one mentally or physically is vulnerable in response to provocation or to particular things in general. As in emotions, stress, or pain.
A distressing or dangerous situation.
definitions.example: He was in trouble when the rain started.

definitions.definition: A difficulty, problem, condition, or action contributing to such a situation.
definitions.example: The trouble was a leaking brake line.   The trouble with that suggestion is that we lack the funds to put it in motion.   The bridge column magnified the trouble with a slight tilt in the wrong direction.

definitions.definition: A violent occurrence or event.
definitions.example: the troubles in Northern Ireland

definitions.definition: Efforts taken or expended, typically beyond the normal required.
definitions.example: It's no trouble for me to edit it.

definitions.definition: A malfunction.
definitions.example: He's been in hospital with some heart trouble.   My old car has engine trouble.

definitions.definition: Liability to punishment; conflict with authority.
definitions.example: He had some trouble with the law.

definitions.definition: A fault or interruption in a stratum.
To disturb, stir up, agitate (a medium, especially water).
To mentally distress; to cause (someone) to be anxious or perplexed.
definitions.example: What she said about narcissism is troubling me.

definitions.definition: In weaker sense: to bother or inconvenience.
definitions.example: I will not trouble you to deliver the letter.

definitions.definition: To take pains to do something.
definitions.example: I won't trouble to post the letter today; I can do it tomorrow.

definitions.definition: To worry; to be anxious.
(Australian rhyming slang) Wife.
Malware that appears to perform or actually performs a desired task for a user while performing a harmful task without the user's knowledge or consent.

Recognition of the distinction between good and evil or between right and wrong; respect for and obedience to the rules of right conduct; the mental disposition or characteristic of behaving in a manner intended to produce morally good results.
A set of social rules, customs, traditions, beliefs, or practices which specify proper, acceptable forms of conduct.
A set of personal guiding principles for conduct or a general notion of how to behave, whether respectable or not.
A lesson or pronouncement which contains advice about proper behavior.
A morality play.
Moral philosophy, the branch of philosophy which studies the grounds and nature of rightness, wrongness, good, and evil.
A particular theory concerning the grounds and nature of rightness, wrongness, good, and evil.
A gathering of people for the purchase and sale of merchandise at a set time, often periodic.
definitions.example: The privilege to hold a weekly market was invaluable for any feudal era burgh.

definitions.definition: City square or other fairly spacious site where traders set up stalls and buyers browse the merchandise.
A grocery store
definitions.example: Stop by the market on your way home and pick up some milk

definitions.definition: A group of potential customers for one's product.
definitions.example: We believe that the market for the new widget is the older homeowner.

definitions.definition: A geographical area where a certain commercial demand exists.
definitions.example: Foreign markets were lost as our currency rose versus their valuta.

definitions.definition: A formally organized, sometimes monopolistic, system of trading in specified goods or effects.
definitions.example: The stock market ceased to be monopolized by the paper-shuffling national stock exchanges with the advent of Internet markets.

definitions.definition: The sum total traded in a process of individuals trading for certain commodities.
The price for which a thing is sold in a market; hence, value; worth.
To make (products or services) available for sale and promote them.
definitions.example: We plan to market an ecology model by next quarter.

definitions.definition: To sell
definitions.example: We marketed more this quarter already than all last year!

definitions.definition: To deal in a market; to buy or sell; to make bargains for provisions or goods.
A multicoloured arch in the sky, produced by prismatic refraction of light within droplets of rain in the air.
Any prismatic refraction of light showing a spectrum of colours.
(often used with “of”) A wide assortment; a varied multitude.
definitions.example: a rainbow of possibilities

definitions.definition: An illusion, mirage.
definitions.example: Many electoral promises are rainbows, vanishing soon after poll day.

definitions.definition: A curveball, particularly a slow one.
In Texas hold 'em or Omaha hold 'em, a flop that contains three different suits.
Rainbow trout.
To brighten with, or as with, a rainbow; to pattern with the colours of the rainbow.
To take the appearance of a rainbow.
Multicolored.
Made up of several races or ethnicities, or (more broadly) of several cultural or ideological factions.
LGBT.
(chiefly of a flop) Composed entirely of different suits.
A pretty person; a term of address to a pretty person.
Something that is pretty.
definitions.example: We'll stop at the knife store and look at the sharp pretties.

definitions.definition: To make pretty; to beautify
Pleasant to the sight or other senses; attractive, especially of women or children, but less strikingly than something beautiful.
Of objects or things: nice-looking, appealing.
Fine-looking; only superficially attractive; initially appealing but having little substance; see petty.
Cunning; clever, skilful.
Moderately large; considerable.
Excellent, commendable, pleasing; fitting or proper (of actions, thoughts etc.).
Awkward, unpleasant.
Somewhat, fairly, quite; sometimes also (by meiosis) very.
Prettily, in a pretty manner.
The science of planting and growing trees in forests.
The art and practice of planting and growing trees in forests.
The art and practice of cultivating, exploiting and renewing forests for commercial purposes.
Commercial tree farming.
A tree farm.
One who writes for the screen, who writes drama for film or television; especially a professional who knows the conventions appropriate to such work.
Likely to be affected by or to experience something.
definitions.example: He's subject to sneezing fits.

definitions.definition: Conditional upon.
definitions.example: The local board sets local policy, subject to approval from the State Board.

definitions.definition: Placed or situated under; lying below, or in a lower situation.
Placed under the power of another; owing allegiance to a particular sovereign or state.
To disagree with or oppose something or someone; (especially in a Court of Law) to raise an objection.
definitions.example: I object to the proposal to build a new airport terminal.

definitions.definition: To offer in opposition as a criminal charge or by way of accusation or reproach; to adduce as an objection or adverse reason.
To set before or against; to bring into opposition; to oppose.

Any of certain marine bivalve mollusks, especially those of the family Ostreidae (the true oysters), usually found adhering to rocks or other fixed objects in shallow water along the seacoasts, or in brackish water in the mouth of rivers.
The delicate morsel of dark meat contained in a small cavity of the bone on each side of the lower part of the back of a fowl.
A pale beige color tinted with grey or pink, like that of an oyster.
(by analogy) A person who keeps secrets.
A shoplifter.
To fish for oysters.
Of a pale beige colour tinted with grey or pink, like that of an oyster.
To cause to ingest (a drug), either by openly offering or through deceit.
definitions.example: We administered the medicine to our dog by mixing it in his food.

definitions.definition: To apportion out, distribute.
To manage or supervise the conduct, performance or execution of; to govern or regulate the parameters for the conduct, performance or execution of; to work in an administrative capacity.
To minister (to).
definitions.example: administering to the sick

definitions.definition: To settle, as the estate of one who dies without a will, or whose will fails of an executor.
To give, as an oath.
To give a drug to a patient, be it orally or by any other means.
The act of depriving, dispossessing, or bereaving; the act of deposing or divesting of some dignity.
The state of being deprived
The taking away from a clergyman of his benefice, or other spiritual promotion or dignity.
(followed by “of”) lack
definitions.example: He was suffering from deprivation of sleep.

definitions.definition: An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.
An amendment which deletes the contents of a bill and inserts entirely new provisions.
Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
The act of producing howls.
definitions.example: The howling of wolves is haunting at night.

definitions.definition: Any of several animals related to Equus ferus caballus.
Equipment with legs.
Type of equipment.

A mass of earthy matter, or rock of the same character as the wall rock, occurring in the course of a vein, as of coal or ore; hence, to take horse (said of a vein) is to divide into branches for a distance.
The sedative, antidepressant, and anxiolytic drug morphine, chiefly when used illicitly.

An informal variant of basketball in which players match shots made by their opponent(s), each miss adding a letter to the word "horse", with 5 misses spelling the whole word and eliminating a player, until only the winner is left. Also HORSE, H-O-R-S-E or H.O.R.S.E. (see Variations of basketball#H-O-R-S-E).
(among students) A translation or other illegitimate aid in study or examination.
(among students) horseplay; tomfoolery
Money or property bequeathed to someone in a will.
Something inherited from a predecessor or the past.
definitions.example: John Muir left as his legacy an enduring spirit of respect for the environment.

definitions.definition: The descendant of an alumnus.
definitions.example: Because she was a legacy, her mother's sorority rushed her.

definitions.definition: Left over from the past; no longer current.
Three times.
A person or animal that runs away or has run away; a person, animal, or organization that escapes captivity or restrictions.
definitions.example: Runaway children are vulnerable to criminal exploitation.

definitions.definition: A vehicle (especially, a train) that is out of control.
(usually attributive) An object or process that is out of control or out of equilibrium.
The act of running away, especially of a horse or teams.
An overwhelming victory.
definitions.example: The home side won in a runaway.

definitions.definition: Having run away; escaped; fugitive
definitions.example: a runaway thief

definitions.definition: Easily won, as a contest
definitions.example: a runaway victory at the polls

definitions.definition: Unchecked; rampant
definitions.example: runaway prices

definitions.definition: Deserting or revolting against one's group, duties, expected conduct, or the like, especially to establish or join a rival group, change one's life drastically, etc.
definitions.example: The runaway delegates nominated their own candidate.

definitions.definition: A disorder of infancy and early childhood caused by a deficiency of vitamin D, causing soft bones.
A long journey, especially by ship.
The act or practice of travelling.
To go on a long journey.
To push or pump (something, especially fluids) into a cavity or passage.
definitions.example: The nurse injected a painkilling drug into the veins of my forearm.

definitions.definition: To introduce (something) suddenly or violently.
definitions.example: Punk injected a much-needed sense of urgency into the British music scene.

definitions.definition: To administer an injection to (someone or something), especially of medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: Now lie back while we inject you with the anesthetic.

definitions.definition: To take or be administered something by means of injection, especially medicine or drugs.
definitions.example: It's been a week since I stopped injecting, and I'm still in withdrawal.

definitions.definition: To introduce (code) into an existing program or its memory space, often without tight integration and sometimes through a security vulnerability.
To cast or throw; used with on.
Sameness, identicalness; the quality or fact of (several specified things) being the same.

The difference or character that marks off an individual or collective from the rest of the same kind, selfhood, sense of who something or someone or oneself is, or the recurring characteristics that enable the recognition of such an individual or group by others or themself.
definitions.example: I've been through so many changes, I have no sense of identity.

definitions.definition: A name or persona—a mask or appearance one presents to the world—by which one is known.
definitions.example: This criminal has taken on several identities.

definitions.definition: An equation which always holds true regardless of the choice of input variables.
Any function which maps all elements of its domain to themselves.
An element of an algebraic structure which, when applied to another element under an operation in that structure, yields this second element.
A well-known or famous person.
definitions.example: 2013 April 4, "Cricket identities consult lawyers", New Zealand Herald

definitions.definition: One who is not susceptible to death.
A member of an elite regiment of the Persian army.
A member of the Académie française.
An administrator of a multi-user dungeon; a wizard.
Not susceptible to death; living forever; never dying.
Never to be forgotten; that merits being always remembered.
definitions.example: his immortal words

definitions.definition: Connected with or relating to immortality.
Exceedingly great; excessive; grievous.
An aquatic or marine carnivorous mammal in the subfamily Lutrinae of the family Mustelidae, which also includes weasels, polecats, badgers, and others.
A hairy man with a slender physique, in contrast with a bear, who is more thickset.
A person who builds or constructs things.
Master artisan, who receives his instructions from the architect, and employs workers.
A bodybuilder.
A solid or hollow sphere, or roughly spherical mass.
definitions.example: a ball of spittle; a fecal ball

definitions.definition: A round or ellipsoidal object.
(mildly, usually in the plural) A testicle.
A leather-covered cushion, fastened to a handle called a ballstock; formerly used by printers for inking the form, then superseded by the roller.
A large pill, a form in which medicine was given to horses; a bolus.
To form or wind into a ball.
definitions.example: to ball cotton

definitions.definition: To heat in a furnace and form into balls for rolling.
To have sexual intercourse with.
To gather balls which cling to the feet, as of damp snow or clay; to gather into balls.
definitions.example: The horse balls; the snow balls.

definitions.definition: (usually in present participle) To be hip or cool.
To play basketball.
To punish by affixing a ball and chain

An appeal by the crowd for holding the ball against a tackled player. This is heard almost any time an opposition player is tackled, without regard to whether the rules about "prior opportunity" to dispose of the ball are fulfilled.
To declare openly and boldly, as something believed to be right; to own, acknowledge or confess frankly.
To bind or devote by a vow.
To acknowledge and justify, as an act done. See avowry.
Openly acknowledged
Positively stated
Asserted under oath, or vow
Relating to motion
Relating to kinesis or motor function
An excursion that lasts most of the day.
To go on a one-day excursion; To visit someplace and return within a day.
A European songbird, Luscinia megarhynchos, of the family Muscicapidae.
definitions.example: Nightingales have been spotted in this coppice.

definitions.definition: A blow; a punch; a striking against; the collision of one body against another; the stroke that touches anything.
definitions.example: The hit was very slight.

definitions.definition: Something very successful, such as a song, film, or video game, that receives widespread recognition and acclaim.
An attack on a location, person or people.
A collision of a projectile with the target.
A match found by searching a computer system or search engine
A measured visit to a web site, a request for a single file from a web server.
An approximately correct answer in a test set.
The complete play, when the batter reaches base without the benefit of a walk, error, or fielder’s choice.
definitions.example: The catcher got a hit to lead off the fifth.

definitions.definition: A dose of an illegal or addictive drug.
definitions.example: Where am I going to get my next hit?

definitions.definition: A premeditated murder done for criminal or political purposes.
A peculiarly apt expression or turn of thought; a phrase which hits the mark.
definitions.example: a happy hit

definitions.definition: A move that throws one of the opponent's men back to the entering point.
A game won after the adversary has removed some of his men. It counts for less than a gammon.
(heading, physical) To strike.
To manage to touch (a target) in the right place.
definitions.example: I hit the jackpot.

definitions.definition: To switch on.
definitions.example: Somebody's been here! Hit the lights!

definitions.definition: To briefly visit.
definitions.example: We hit the grocery store on the way to the park.

definitions.definition: To encounter an obstacle or other difficulty.
definitions.example: You'll hit some nasty thunderstorms if you descend too late.  We hit a lot of traffic coming back from the movies.

definitions.definition: (heading) To attain, to achieve.
To affect negatively.
definitions.example: The economy was hit by a recession.  The hurricane hit his fishing business hard.

definitions.definition: To attack.
(heading, games) To make a play.
To use; to connect to.
definitions.example: The external web servers hit DBSRV7, but the internal web server hits DBSRV3.

definitions.definition: To have sex with.
definitions.example: I'd hit that.

definitions.definition: To inhale an amount of smoke from a narcotic substance, particularly marijuana.
The heading or title of a magazine or newspaper article.
definitions.example: The headline on today's newspaper reads "John Doe Wins Wood-Splitting Competition."

definitions.definition: The line at the top of a page containing the folio or number of the page.
(entertainment) The top-billed attraction.
A headrope.
(entertainment) To have top billing; to be the main attraction.
